rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Assumptions and guarantees referencing component ports must be in component type scopes; implementation-level bindings go in implementation scopes.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structural integrity when inserting AGREE annexes; ensure all referenced identifiers exist in the model.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE contracts.
Do not assume that AADL properties are automatically available as AGREE variables; they must be explicitly represented.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type scopes are for general assumptions and guarantees; implementation scopes are for specific bindings.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or vice versa.
Do not reference AADL properties like Period or Deadline directly in AGREE expressions without explicit model representation.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
AADL annex placement rules beyond scope
